name: Nouvelle-Aquitaine (France)

colors.symbolism: the principal colour of the regional logo
the secondary colour of the regional logo

description: A white field bearing the institutional logo of the Region Nouvelle-Aquitaine in blue and red.
history: Nouvelle-Aquitaine is a French region created by the territorial reform of 2016, merging Aquitaine, Limousin, and Poitou-Charentes. France traditionally does not promote regional flags, and the regional council uses an institutional logo flag in a de-facto capacity rather than an officially adopted heraldic banner.

features.symbolism: A white field bearing the institutional logo of the Nouvelle-Aquitaine region in blue and red.

features.role: the institutional logo of the Region Nouvelle-Aquitaine, a stylised abstract device in blue and red
features.symbolism: The logo of the Region Nouvelle-Aquitaine, used by the regional council since the 2016 territorial reform that merged Aquitaine, Limousin, and Poitou-Charentes.
